data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>`.** Here's the working syntax:

```csharp
Span<nint> span = stackalloc nint[4];
```

It compiled and ran successfully, allocating 4 native-sized integers (8 bytes each on this 64-bit system) on the stack. This works because `nint` is an [unmanaged type](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/builtin-types/unmanaged-types), which is the requirement for `stackalloc`. The same applies to `nuint`.
